# identity-upkeep

## Purpose

Keep the identity repository accurate, minimal, and bounded so it defines who or what is participating without turning into an inferred biography or noisy memory dump.

## Trigger

Use this skill when:
- identity files no longer match current roles, boundaries, or working preferences
- new identity-relevant constraints are explicitly confirmed
- identity and project details have started to blur together
- a privacy or boundary review is due

## Inputs

- the current identity repository docs and indexes
- explicit user-confirmed identity facts or preference changes
- relevant teleology doctrine about privacy, memory, and authority
- any repo-local notes that may have been misplaced into identity

## Procedure

1. Re-read the identity entrypoints and identify what the layer currently claims about participants, roles, preferences, principles, and constraints.
2. Remove or relocate content that does not belong here. Project facts go to project space, source facts go to source indexes, and reusable procedures go to skills.
3. Keep only explicit, identity-native information. Do not infer personality, goals, or biography from project work unless the user has confirmed it belongs here.
4. Normalize the layer toward durable operating identity: communication preferences, boundary rules, participation roles, and stable working constraints.
5. Check for privacy leaks or over-specific detail that should not be broadly propagated.
6. Record any identity-meaningful change in the relevant log or linked note.

## Verification

- identity files contain identity-native context rather than project facts
- no important preferences or boundaries are only implicit
- privacy-sensitive or inferential material has been excluded or minimized
- the layer is compact enough that future agents can read it quickly

## Failure Modes

- identity becomes a mixed dump of preferences, project facts, and guesses
- agents infer identity from behavior instead of explicit confirmation
- privacy boundaries erode through convenience edits
- useful working constraints are lost because nobody maintains the layer
